title: Substitute (`:s`)
subtitle: The most powerful single command in Vim.
summary: `:s/pattern/replacement/flags` is the workhorse for text transformation. Combine with ranges (`%` for whole file) and flags (`g` for global, `c` for confirm, `i` for case-insensitive).

blocks.text:
`:s` is Vim's find-and-replace. The full form is:

```
:[range]s/pattern/replacement/flags
```

Master it and you can rewrite a file in one keystroke.

rows: All matches in last visual selection
Replace all matches on each line (not just first)
Don't substitute â€” just count matches

blocks.title: What `:s` Actually Does
blocks.text:
`:s` runs the regex engine over each line in `[range]` exactly once (unless `g` flag), substituting matches in place. The buffer's lines are rewritten line-by-line; the cursor lands on the last line changed.

  â€˘ Without `[range]`, the range is the **current line only** (this trips up beginners â€” they expect file-wide and get one line).
  â€˘ The `%` range is shorthand for `1,$` (whole file).
  â€˘ The `c` flag enters an interactive prompt â€” `y` = yes, `n` = no, `a` = all (rest), `q` = quit, `l` = last (do this then quit).

**Re-running:** `:&` repeats the last substitute on the current line. `:%&` repeats on the whole file. {key:&} in *normal mode* = same as `:&`.
`:%s//bar/g` reuses the last search pattern as the substitute pattern. Pair with {key:*} (search word under cursor) for a 5-keystroke replace-all.

blocks.title: Substitute is search-pattern + replacement-template + flags
blocks.text: `:s` composes three subsystems: the regex engine (same one driving {key:/}); a small templating layer that expands `\0`, `\1`â€“`\9`, `&`, `~`, `\u`, `\U`, `\l`, `\L`, `\E`; and a flag-driven loop controller (`g`, `c`, `i`, `e`, `n`, `p`, `&`). Empty pattern reuses the last search â€” that's the global search-pattern slot again.
